children.name: Cervus
children.description: The red deer and their relatives — including the wapiti, sika deer, and Bactrian deer — a genus of large deer distributed across Europe, Asia, and North America, with males bearing large branching antlers shed and regrown annually.

children.name: Cervus elaphus
children.description: Cervus elaphus (Red deer) is a red deer species found in Europe and Asia regions.

children.name: Cervus canadensis
children.description: Cervus canadensis (Elk (Wapiti)) is a red deer species found in North America and Asia regions.

children.name: Cervus nippon
children.description: Cervus nippon (Sika deer) is a red deer species found in Asia and Europe regions.

children.name: Cervus hanglu
children.description: Cervus hanglu (Hangul (Kashmir deer)) is a red deer species found in Asia region.

children.name: Dama
children.description: The fallow deer, one or two species native to the Mediterranean region and the Middle East, characterised by distinctive palmate antlers in males and a spotted summer coat, widely introduced to parks and forests worldwide.

children.name: Dama dama
children.description: Dama dama (Fallow deer) is a fallow deer species found in Europe and Asia and North America and South America and Oceania regions.

children.name: Dama mesopotamica
children.description: Dama mesopotamica (Persian fallow deer) is a fallow deer species found in Asia region.

children.name: Axis
children.description: The axis deer (chital) and its close relatives, a small genus of South Asian deer known for retaining their spotted coats into adulthood, making the chital one of the most visually striking deer in the world.

children.name: Axis axis
children.description: Axis axis (Chital (Spotted deer)) is a fallow deer species found in Asia region.

children.name: Axis porcinus
children.description: Axis porcinus (Hog deer) is a fallow deer species found in Asia region.

children.name: Rusa
children.description: The rusa deer, four species of large deer native to Indonesia, the Philippines, and the western Pacific, including the Javan rusa and Timor deer, widely introduced across the Pacific and Australia.

children.name: Rusa unicolor
children.description: Rusa unicolor (Sambar) is a sambar species found in Asia region.

children.name: Rusa timorensis
children.description: Rusa timorensis (Timor deer) is a sambar species found in Asia and Oceania regions.

children.name: Rusa marianna
children.description: Rusa marianna (Philippine sambar) is a sambar species found in Asia region.

children.name: Rusa alfredi
children.description: Rusa alfredi (Visayan spotted deer) is a sambar species found in Asia region.

children.name: Rucervus
children.description: The swamp deer and barasingha, three endangered South Asian deer of floodplains and wet grasslands, including the barasingha whose name means 'twelve-tined' in reference to the male's impressive antlers.

children.name: Rucervus eldii
children.description: Rucervus eldii (Eld's deer (Thamin)) is a sambar species found in Asia region.

children.name: Rucervus duvaucelii
children.description: Rucervus duvaucelii (Barasingha (Swamp deer)) is a sambar species found in Asia region.

children.name: Rucervus schomburgki
children.description: Rucervus schomburgki (Schomburgk's deer) is a sambar species found in Asia region.

children.name: Elaphurus
children.description: A monotypic genus containing Père David's deer, a large Chinese deer extinct in the wild for centuries and known only from a captive herd in the imperial hunting grounds, now successfully reintroduced to China through conservation breeding.

children.name: Elaphurus davidianus
children.description: Elaphurus davidianus (Père David's deer) is a muntjac species found in Asia region.

children.name: Przewalskium
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the white-lipped deer, a large, high-altitude deer of the Tibetan Plateau and Qinghai, named for the distinctive white muzzle contrasting with its dark coat.

children.name: Przewalskium albirostris
children.description: Przewalskium albirostris (White-lipped deer) is a sambar species found in Asia region.

children.name: Muntiacus
children.description: The muntjacs, around 12 small to medium-sized deer of South and Southeast Asia, among the most ancient living deer, with males bearing short spike-like antlers and prominent facial tusk-like canines used in territorial fights.

children.name: Muntiacus muntjak
children.description: Muntiacus muntjak (Indian muntjac) is a muntjac species found in Asia region.

children.name: Muntiacus reevesi
children.description: Muntiacus reevesi (Reeve's muntjac) is a muntjac species found in Asia and Europe regions.

children.name: Muntiacus vuquangensis
children.description: Muntiacus vuquangensis (Giant muntjac) is a muntjac species found in Asia region.

children.name: Muntiacus putaoensis
children.description: Muntiacus putaoensis (Leaf muntjac) is a muntjac species found in Asia region.

children.name: Muntiacus rooseveltorum
children.description: Muntiacus rooseveltorum (Roosevelt's muntjac) is a muntjac species found in Asia region.

children.name: Elaphodus
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the tufted deer, a small Chinese deer recognisable by its prominent dark hair tuft on the forehead and the male's elongated sabre-like canine teeth, inhabiting dense mountain forests.

children.name: Elaphodus cephalophus
children.description: Elaphodus cephalophus (Tufted deer) is a muntjac species found in Asia region.

children.name: Capreolus
children.description: The roe deer, two closely related small deer of Europe and Siberia, the most widespread deer on the continent, known for their capacity to delay embryo implantation (embryonic diapause) to synchronise births with spring.

children.name: Capreolus capreolus
children.description: Capreolus capreolus (European roe deer) is a roe deer species found in Europe and Asia regions.

children.name: Capreolus pygargus
children.description: Capreolus pygargus (Siberian roe deer) is a roe deer species found in Asia region.

children.name: Alces
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the moose (elk in Europe), the largest living deer, with males bearing enormous palmate antlers spanning up to 1.8 m, inhabiting boreal forests and tundra across the Northern Hemisphere.

children.name: Alces alces
children.description: Alces alces (Moose (Älg)) is a moose species found in Europe and Asia and North America regions.

children.name: Rangifer
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the reindeer (caribou), the only deer species in which both sexes grow antlers, circumpolar in distribution across Arctic and subarctic tundra, and the only cervid to have been widely domesticated.

children.name: Rangifer tarandus
children.description: Rangifer tarandus (Reindeer / Caribou) is a reindeer species found in Europe and Asia and North America regions.

children.name: Odocoileus
children.description: The white-tailed and mule deer, two of North America's most abundant large mammals, the white-tailed deer being the most numerous wild ungulate on the continent and a cornerstone of many forest ecosystems.

children.name: Odocoileus virginianus
children.description: Odocoileus virginianus (White-tailed deer) is a american deer species found in North America and South America regions.

children.name: Odocoileus hemionus
children.description: Odocoileus hemionus (Mule deer) is a american deer species found in North America region.

children.name: Mazama
children.description: The brocket deer, around seven small to medium-sized South and Central American deer of dense tropical forests, with simple spike antlers and a hunched posture adapted to moving through dense understorey vegetation.

children.name: Mazama americana
children.description: Mazama americana (Red brocket) is a american deer species found in South America and North America regions.

children.name: Mazama gouazoubira
children.description: Mazama gouazoubira (Gray brocket) is a american deer species found in South America region.

children.name: Mazama nemorivaga
children.description: Mazama nemorivaga (Amazonian brown brocket) is a american deer species found in South America region.

children.name: Mazama chunyi
children.description: Mazama chunyi (Peruvian dwarf brocket) is a american deer species found in South America region.

children.name: Mazama temama
children.description: Mazama temama (Central American red brocket) is a american deer species found in North America and South America regions.

children.name: Mazama bororo
children.description: Mazama bororo (Small red brocket) is a american deer species found in South America region.

children.name: Blastocerus
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the marsh deer, the largest South American deer, inhabiting flooded grasslands and riverine wetlands of the Pantanal and Paraná basin, with large splayed hooves adapted to soft, waterlogged ground.

children.name: Blastocerus dichotomus
children.description: Blastocerus dichotomus (Marsh deer) is a american deer species found in South America region.

children.name: Ozotoceros
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the pampas deer, a medium-sized South American deer of open grasslands and savannas, now greatly reduced in range due to habitat loss across the Argentine pampas and Brazilian cerrado.

children.name: Ozotoceros bezoarticus
children.description: Ozotoceros bezoarticus (Pampas deer) is a american deer species found in South America region.

children.name: Hippocamelus
children.description: The huemul and taruca, two endangered Andean deer of steep rocky terrain in South America, with stocky builds and short legs suited to navigating rugged Andean slopes and ravines at high altitude.

children.name: Hippocamelus antisensis
children.description: Hippocamelus antisensis (North Andean huemul) is a american deer species found in South America region.

children.name: Hippocamelus bisulcus
children.description: Hippocamelus bisulcus (South Andean huemul) is a american deer species found in South America region.

children.name: Pudu
children.description: The pudús, two species that are the world's smallest deer, native to dense temperate rainforests and Andean slopes of South America, with a shy, solitary lifestyle and simple spike antlers barely exceeding 10 cm.

children.name: Pudu puda
children.description: Pudu puda (Southern pudu) is a american deer species found in South America region.

children.name: Pudu mephistophiles
children.description: Pudu mephistophiles (Northern pudu) is a american deer species found in South America region.

children.name: Hydropotes
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the water deer, an unusual Chinese and Korean deer that lacks antlers entirely and instead bears long, curved canine tusks in both sexes, and is the only cervid known to give birth to up to seven fawns.

children.name: Hydropotes inermis
children.description: Hydropotes inermis (Water deer) is a water deer species found in Asia and Europe regions.
